name: CSG UV And Corner Fixes

messages.content:
I’ll switch from debugging to the branch-management request now. First I’m checking the current branch, worktree state, and remotes so I don’t lose the uncommitted CSG/AVBOIT work or accidentally delete the wrong branch.

The branch to merge is `codex/csg-preserve-uv-attributes`, and the only uncommitted files are local `.cozter` state, uploaded screenshots/PDF text, and `__artifacts/`. I’m going to stash those temporarily, merge the clean branch into `main`, push `main`, delete the feature branch locally and remotely, then restore the local files on `main`.

The feature branch is clean and contains three CSG commits on top of `main`: UV preservation, corner-side subtraction, and seam attribute preservation. I’m fetching and merging now; this should be a fast-forward unless `origin/main` moved.

`origin/main` moved while this branch was open. It already contains an earlier PR merge plus an AVBOIT commit, so this merge will bring only the two newer CSG commits from the feature branch onto current `main`. A `.cozter` schedule file changed again locally during the fetch, so I’m stashing that separately before switching branches.

The merge into `main` completed cleanly. I’m pushing `main` now, then I’ll remove the feature branch from both local git and `origin`.

`main` is now pushed at `51cffd2`, and the local/remote feature branch is gone. The workspace is on `main`; only `.cozter` local state is dirty again, so I’m checking the temporary stashes before restoring the uploaded files/artifacts without touching the committed source.

I restored the uploaded files and artifact directory from the temporary stash onto `main`. I’m doing a final verification now: branch, pushed commit, local/remote branch deletion, and remaining local-only files.

One cleanup issue showed up: restoring the untracked upload folder made git mark a few older tracked `.cozter/uploads` files as deleted. I’m restoring those tracked local-state files from `main` so the merge/push result stays clean for source files.

Done.

`main` is checked out and pushed at `51cffd2`:
`Merge branch 'codex/csg-preserve-uv-attributes'`

I deleted `codex/csg-preserve-uv-attributes` both locally and from `origin`; remote verification returned no branch.

Current worktree has no dirty source files. Only local `.cozter` state, uploaded screenshots/PDF, and `__artifacts/` remain uncommitted/preserved. No tests were run in this turn; this was only the branch merge/delete/switch operation.
merge the branch to main and remove this branch and switch to main

I’ll do the branch cleanup now: verify the current state, protect any uncommitted local artifacts, fast-forward or merge into `main`, push, delete the feature branch, then leave the workspace on `main`.

The workspace is already on `main`, and the local feature branch is not present in `git branch -vv`. I’m fetching/pruning now to verify whether the remote branch still exists before deleting anything.

`main` already contains the merge commit for `codex/csg-preserve-uv-attributes`, and `origin/main` is at the same commit. I’m doing one final push/status check and verifying there is no leftover local or remote feature branch.

Done. The workspace is on `main`, and `main` is synced with `origin/main` at `3ef9d72`.

`codex/csg-preserve-uv-attributes` is already merged into `main` and no longer exists locally or on `origin`. I also pushed `main`; Git reported `Everything up-to-date`.

I left unrelated local state untouched:
- `.cozter/sessions/c93aeca0e5be.json`
- `__artifacts/`
- existing stashes, since they may contain local session state.
show me a fresh screenshot of CSG on a corner

I’ll generate a new corner-case CSG capture from the current `main`, using the compositor/full-frame path rather than the older X11 capture route. I’ll first locate the existing smoke/capture harness and confirm the target/operator/camera keys before running it.

The repo is on `main` and synced, with only a local `.cozter` session deletion showing as dirty. I’m leaving that alone and using the existing smoke capture script so the screenshot reflects the current built testbed path.

The testbed capture script can select the CSG target, operator, camera preset, click point, and commit the preview. For a visible corner cut I’m going to use the cube target with a cylinder operator and an off-center click near a cube corner, then capture from the right-high camera.

The available `dbg` testbed binary and cooked `testbed/res` are fresh for today, so I’ll capture from that output root. The CMake invocation I tried against the runtime output directory is not a build directory, so I’m not changing binaries for this screenshot run.

I found an existing corner-cube capture, and it used the default CSG click after switching to the `right high` camera: that places the operator across the visible cube corner. I’m regenerating that case now as a fresh artifact: cube target, cylinder operator, committed CSG, right-high angle.

The smoke run completed and confirmed the commit log before capture. I’m converting the BMP to PNG and doing integrity checks plus a quick pixel sanity pass so I don’t attach another visually clipped frame.

Fresh committed CSG corner capture, cube target + cylinder operator, right-high angle. Verified full-frame PNG at `1280x900`.

[[attach: __artifacts/csg_corner_cube_cylinder_right_high_fresh.png]]

also, I think we better split the geometry by:
static, static deform, skinned, skinned deform

I’ll map where geometry is currently represented before making changes. The split you named sounds like a capability classification, so I’m checking whether the code already distinguishes static meshes, deformable meshes, and skinned data.

The current code already has two asset families: `geometry` for ordinary static meshes and `deformable_geometry` for editable/runtime meshes. I’m reading the asset/component/render-system boundaries now to see whether this should become an explicit four-way kind or a directory/type split in assets and runtime components.

This is looking like a cross-product split: “can topology/rest vertices be runtime-mutated?” and “does it require skinning?” Today those concepts are folded together under `DeformableGeometry`, so I’m checking the import/cook path before deciding whether to make the split explicit in metadata or inferred from payload.

I’m going to make this explicit without renaming the existing asset files right now: add a four-way `GeometryClass`, let static `geometry` resolve to `Static`, and let `deformable_geometry` carry `StaticDeform`, `Skinned`, or `SkinnedDeform`. That preserves the current render/runtime paths while making the split visible to the cooker, binary asset, tests, and tools.

I’m about to edit the asset layer and runtime cache. The key choice is to keep the current `geometry`/`deformable_geometry` asset types for compatibility, then add a `geometry_class` value so the four categories are explicit and enforceable.

The code changes are in place now: the class travels from metadata through cooked binary into runtime instances, and surface-edit entry points reject non-deformable classes. I’m moving into compile/test now, starting with the focused assets and ECS graphics targets because they exercise the new split.

The build preset names differ from configure preset names here. I’m switching to the existing `linux-clang-dbg` build preset and targeting only the affected test binaries and converter first.

The focused build passed. I’m running the two affected test binaries now; these cover asset cook/load/codec behavior and the surface-edit runtime path.

The focused tests are green. Because the deformable geometry binary version changed, I’m also rebuilding the testbed/resource path so the cooked assets match the new header before I hand back the screenshot/artifact context.

I’m going to refresh the corner CSG capture once after the binary-version/cooker change, using the rebuilt `full/dbg` testbed from its executable directory so it mounts the fresh `res` volume.

One important detail: several validation-failure checks only compile in the `fin` configuration. I’m running the same focused assets/ECS tests there too so the new class validation does not hide an existing failure mode.

Implemented the geometry split as explicit classes: `static`, `static_deform`, `skinned`, and `skinned_deform`.

Key points:
- Added shared `GeometryClass` helpers and serialization.
- Cooker and FBX metadata now understand `asset.geometry_class`.
- Runtime deformation/CSG rejects non-deform classes.
- `skinned` is reserved for skinned meshes without runtime surface deformation.
- Existing deformable assets infer `static_deform` or `skinned_deform` when metadata is absent.

Verified with:
- `linux-clang-dbg` build for asset/ecs tests, `fbx_to_nwb`, `testbed`, `resource_cooker`
- `linux-clang-fin` asset/ecs tests
- `assets_graphics_tests` and `ecs_graphics_tests` passing in both checked configurations
- Fresh CSG corner screenshot after the split

I left the unrelated `.cozter` local state changes alone.

[[attach: __artifacts/csg_corner_cube_cylinder_right_high_after_geometry_split.png]]

I’ll check the current branch and staged/untracked changes first, then commit only the relevant workspace changes and push that branch. If the dirty files are just local artifacts or session state, I’ll call that out instead of bundling them into a code commit.

There are real source changes on `main`, plus local `.cozter` session files. I’m going to inspect the diff at a high level, exclude the local session state, run a focused verification if practical, then commit and push the source changes.

The diff is the expected geometry classification work: a new shared `GeometryClass`, cooker/importer metadata, runtime cache checks, and tests. I’m checking the exact behavior and available test targets now so the commit message matches the change and I don’t push an unverified build break.

The change writes the class into deformable geometry binary version 8, rejects mismatched skin/deform payloads, and exposes static/skinned/deform distinctions through the converter. I’m going to run the relevant asset and ECS graphics test binaries if they are available in this checkout.

The engine preset does not expose an `assets_graphics_tests` target under that name, so I’m checking the generated target list and will build/run the actual test targets that cover these files.

The full Linux preset has the asset graphics test target; I found the exact generated targets. I’m building the affected debug tests there now, then I’ll run them from `__exec/linux/x64/full/dbg`.

Both affected debug test suites passed. I’m staging the source/test/converter files explicitly, leaving `.cozter` local state untouched, then I’ll commit and push `main`.

Committed the source changes as `98e62ce` on `main`. I’m pushing now, then I’ll do a final status check to make sure only the local `.cozter` files remain dirty.

Committed and pushed to `origin/main`.

Commit: `98e62ce Split geometry classes`

Validation passed:
- `assets_graphics_tests`
- `ecs_graphics_tests`

I left the local `.cozter` session files uncommitted.

check if there's any backward compability code and remove them.
once you've done your job, commit and push

I’ll audit the pending geometry-classification changes for compatibility shims before committing. First I’m checking the current branch, dirty files, and diff so I can separate real source changes from local artifacts.

The code tree is currently clean apart from `.cozter` local session files, so there isn’t a pending source diff to trim. I’m searching the checked-in code for explicit backward-compatibility paths, legacy aliases, and geometry-class migration fallbacks now.

The first clear compatibility shim is in the FBX converter: it accepts the old `deformable_geometry` type string and rewrites it to `static_deform`. I’m checking the cooker and tests next, because optional `geometry_class` defaults can also be a schema-compatibility path rather than strict new behavior.

The cooker currently treats `geometry_class` as optional and infers the old behavior from the asset payload. That is effectively backward compatibility for old `.nwb` metadata, so I’m going to make `geometry_class` required and update the in-repo metadata/tests to be explicit.

I found two actual shims to remove: old FBX converter aliases (`geometry` and `deformable_geometry`) and cooker inference when `asset.geometry_class` is omitted. I’m editing those out and then making every in-tree metadata fixture declare the class explicitly so the stricter loader has no hidden defaults.

The metadata fixtures are now explicit, and I added regression cases that old metadata without `geometry_class` fails instead of being inferred. I’m doing a tighter search through the touched areas for remaining aliases/default inference before building.

The remaining matches are normal asset type names or unrelated material variant aliases, not compatibility shims. I’m building the touched targets now: the asset cooker/tests plus the FBX converter that writes `geometry_class`.

The focused build passed. I’m running the asset graphics tests, then I’ll run the cooker against the updated Testbed metadata so the stricter required field is exercised on real assets too.

The first manual cooker invocation used a repo-relative executable path from inside the output directory, so the shell couldn’t find it. I’m rerunning it with absolute paths; no source changes were made by that failed command.

The debug tests and a real Testbed asset cook are green. Since the new missing-field regression is compiled under the final configuration, I’m building and running `fin` for the same focused targets before committing.

The final configuration also passed, including the new required-field checks, and the final Testbed cook succeeded. I’m doing whitespace/status checks now and then I’ll commit only the source/asset changes, leaving local `.cozter` state out.

Debug and final are clean. I’m also running the optimized focused build/test pass because the asset cooker metadata touches Testbed assets used across all three output configs.

All three focused configs built, the asset graphics tests passed, and the Testbed cook succeeded for `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt`. I’m staging only the touched source/test/asset files now; `.cozter` remains unstaged local state.

The staged set is clean and contains only the intended files. I’m committing this as an explicit geometry-class schema change, then I’ll push `main`.

The commit is `cf1da25`, and the only remaining dirty files are the pre-existing local `.cozter` session files. I’m pushing `main` now.

Done and pushed to `main`.

Commit: `cf1da25 Require explicit geometry class metadata`

What changed:
- Removed old FBX converter aliases for `geometry` and `deformable_geometry`.
- Made `asset.geometry_class` required in the asset cooker instead of inferred.
- Removed the deformable class inference helper.
- Updated Testbed `.nwb` assets/tests to declare explicit geometry classes.
- Added validation coverage for missing `geometry_class`.

Verified:
- `assets_graphics_tests`: `dbg`, `fin`, `opt` passed.
- `resource_cooker`: `dbg`, `fin`, `opt` Testbed cooks passed.
- `git diff --cached --check` passed before commit.

Workspace is synced with `origin/main`. Only local `.cozter` session files remain dirty and were not committed.

summary:
Fixed a half-painted window-capture issue: the original `step5_operator_remesh_commit_welded.png` was structurally valid but visually clipped; regenerated a verified full-frame compositor capture as `__artifacts/step5_operator_remesh_commit_welded_full.png` and standardized running from `__exec/linux/x64/full` to avoid stale `res` mounts. Attempted multi-geometry/operator/angle captures; non-plane targets initially failed preview/commit at default hit points, so produced a plane-only comparison contact sheet (`__artifacts/step5_variants_plane_contact_sheet.png`) and treated non-plane rejections as real failures rather than “examples.”

Audited/fixed multiple CSG issues, then committed and pushed `9143629` to `origin/main` (depth gating for closed geometry, preview/commit validity alignment, smoke race fix, removed unused transient renderer API) with builds/tests/cooker smokes green across `dbg/fin/opt`. Later addressed UV distortion on unrelated faces by preserving pre-existing vertex attributes/provenance and added a regression; pushed commits on `codex/csg-preserve-uv-attributes` (notably `9b32e12`) plus a corner-subtract fix (`e32225d`). Merged that branch into `main`, pushed, deleted the branch, and left only `.cozter/` + `__artifacts/` uncommitted. Generated a fresh corner CSG screenshot: `__artifacts/csg_corner_cube_cylinder_right_high_fresh.png`.
long_term: Deformable “subtract” has two paths: a legacy topology/centroid-driven delete+boundary-extrude path and a Step 5 explicit-operator remesh path that clips/splits surface triangles to form a true rim loop (still not a full 3D boolean/remesh).
Preview and commit use the same normalized operator payload, and commit rejects any preview/commit payload mismatch.
Operator meshes are first-class NWB assets under `Testbed/assets/meshes/` and the testbed renders previews from these, positioned/oriented by the preview hit frame.

The operator footprint is derived from the operator mesh geometry’s top-down XY silhouette (`BuildOperatorFootprintFromGeometry()`), so editing the NWB operator mesh changes the committed subtract footprint after recooking assets.

Operator meshes provide a normalized depth/profile used to shape generated walls/caps and to gate/remesh triangle selection against the operator’s 3D volume, including rejecting triangles fully outside the cutter depth on closed geometry.
`PreviewHole()` must only advertise previews that can be committed by validating the same boundary/removal/cap viability plan the commit path requires.
In the Step 5 remesh path, tangent-frame rebuild is opportunistic so commits aren’t rejected solely due to imported asset frame/UV issues when the remesh plan itself is valid.
CSG remesh must preserve authored attributes on untouched surface vertices (UV/normal/tangent/color and provenance/source samples), while only generated cut/wall vertices receive new attributes.
Corner cuts handle perpendicular/degenerate-projection faces via auxiliary 3D/depth-aware clipping while keeping the planar hit-surface rim loop as the wall/cap boundary.
Bottom-cap construction prioritizes watertight welding by reusing the deepest wall ring, and cap triangulation preserves collinear boundary vertices to avoid side/cap open seams.
Launching `testbed` from the repo root can mount a stale sibling `dbg/res`; running from `__exec/linux/x64/testbed` or `__exec/linux/x64/full` mounts freshly cooked `res` first.
Screenshot artifacts should use a compositor-aware capture path (avoiding half-painted X11 grabs) and only be attached after the capture process exits and the PNG is fully written and verified.
